preflight.note: Created before implementation work starts for RSS/Atom signal hardening and roadmap follow-up derived from CLV-018 records.

acceptance_criteria.text: Review completed work item 008 and current signal implementation to identify gaps against CLV-018 roadmap expectations without duplicating already-shipped RSS/Atom behavior.
Define whether RSS/Atom remains a trusted signal lane only or gains any channel-adapter surface; document the boundary so feeds are not treated as person-to-person messages.
Add optional authenticated-feed planning or implementation only if credentials can use secure storage and diagnostics redaction without leaking URL tokens or HTTP credentials.
Harden feed parsing/fetch behavior as needed for redirects, content-type handling, oversized payloads, duplicate IDs, stale feeds, tracking URLs, and malformed XML/Atom fixtures.
Ensure feed-derived items cannot gain approval, urgent-interrupt, or quick-action authority without a separate trusted-source policy and explicit local confirmation.
Improve UI if needed for feed health, mute/retention state, source trust, fetch errors, archive behavior, and clear separation from contacts and private agent requests.
Ensure backup/export/retention and diagnostics behavior for signal subscriptions/items remains covered and redacted where appropriate.
Document shipped RSS/Atom behavior versus future authenticated or action-bearing feed ideas.
